examples: Go in the back door of the house.

definitions.text: Returned or restored to a previous place or condition.
examples: He was on vacation, but now he’s back.
The office fell into chaos when you left, but now order is back.
I’d like to find a back issue of that magazine.

definitions.text: To or in a previous condition or place.
examples: I left my mobile phone back at the hotel. I’ll have to go back and get it.

definitions.text: In a direction opposite to that in which someone or something is facing or normally pointing.
examples: Someone pushed me in the chest and I fell back.
The grandfather clock toppled back and crashed to the ground.

definitions.text: In a direction opposite to the usual or desired direction of movement or progress, physically or figuratively.
examples: Wind the film back a few frames.
Don’t forget to put the clocks back by one hour tonight!

definitions.text: The rear of the body, especially the part between the neck and the end of the spine and opposite the chest and belly.
examples: Could you please scratch my back?
I hurt my back lifting those crates.
I hurt my back lifting those crates.

definitions.text: To go in the reverse direction.
examples: The train backed into the station.
I back you all the way.
Which horse are you backing in this race?

definitions.text: To change direction contrary to the normal pattern; that is, to shift anticlockwise in the northern hemisphere, or clockwise in the southern hemisphere.
A large shallow vat; a cistern, tub, or trough, used by brewers, distillers, dyers, picklers, gluemakers, and others, for mixing or cooling wort, holding water, hot glue, etc.
